product_disposition.reason: Public material names the report but does not publish its footprint, payload, datum details, accuracy, or reuse terms.
The dam-safety report explicitly applies this terrain database to a roughly 120 km downstream dam-break model, outside the upstream playable reach.
The approximately 5 cm DSM is a local dam-site survey and no public evidence places it within Boiling Pot to Mukuni Beach.
World Bank completion reporting attributes the additional LiDAR to the Kariba Dam Rehabilitation Project, not the upstream Batoka game reach.

observed_evidence: Official BGHES project page publishes the final ESIA document family and shared annexures.
The page establishes ZRA as the appropriate first acquisition contact for project survey products.

discovered_sources.provider: Zambezi River Authority / Studio Pietrangeli / ERM-hosted BGHES documents
observed_evidence: Section E.8.2 states that a 0.3 m LiDAR DTM and 30 m SRTM DEM were used.
The LiDAR DTM is described as covering the main Zambezi channel and close surroundings.
The documented coordinate reference is WGS 84 / UTM zone 35S.
Sections E.8.1 through E.8.3 explicitly apply the terrain database to a roughly 120 km dam-break model running downstream from the proposed Batoka dam to Lake Kariba.
The proposed dam is downstream of the complete Boiling Pot to Mukuni Beach game reach, so this published 0.3 m terrain product is not evidence of playable-reach coverage.

discovered_sources.provider: Zambezi River Authority / BGHES feasibility design team
observed_evidence: The published feasibility-design volume list names Vol.3 as 230 GEN R SP 001 - LIDAR TOPOGRAPHIC SURVEY REPORT.
No direct public download for Vol.3, its coverage index, or its terrain payload was found during this review.
Public documents do not establish whether Vol.3 covers the upstream game reach, only the dam site, the future reservoir, the downstream study, or some combination of those areas.
Vol.3 must remain distinct from the downstream 0.3 m dam-break terrain database until report identifiers and footprints prove otherwise.
The Batoka case study reports a roughly 13 square kilometre drone survey at the project area and a DSM at approximately 5 cm ground resolution.
The public paper describes a dam-site engineering comparison rather than upstream full-reach capture.
The paper grants neither access nor reuse rights for source photos, point clouds, DSM, orthophotos, or meshes.

discovered_sources.provider: World Bank / Zambezi River Basin Development Project P133380
observed_evidence: Project P133380 funded engineering studies for the proposed Batoka Gorge HES.
The project completion report says the dam-break analysis was completed under the Kariba project and attributes additional LiDAR to the Kariba Dam Rehabilitation Project.
That separate downstream LiDAR activity is not evidence of Boiling Pot to Mukuni Beach coverage and must not be conflated with feasibility Vol.3.
The public project and document records do not expose a LiDAR point cloud, DTM, orthophoto, breakline package, coverage polygon, or redistribution license.
The public catalog returned 65 thematic layers plus station and background groups without login.
The only listed elevation product is a 1 km Globe layer; Major rivers and Tributaries are generalized basin context.
No Batoka LiDAR, high-resolution DTM or DSM, orthophoto, breakline, surveyed centerline, or project-specific terrain layer is listed.
The portal configuration disallows time-series download because ZAMCOM does not own those records; spatial-product license and redistribution terms are not stated.

contact_routes.role: official project owner and first survey-custodian contact
published Batoka engineering and drone-photogrammetry survey-team contact
official basin data-system custodian and referral route for unlisted survey holdings
formal records request route for project P133380 survey reports, deliverables, coverage, and disposition

outreach_status.reason: sender identity, organization, reply address, and authority to accept quoted data/license cost require project-owner confirmation

priority_contacts: Zambezi River Authority BGHES project office
Zambezi Watercourse Commission ZAMWIS data custodian
World Bank project P133380 records through Access to Information
request_products: Vol.3 230 GEN R SP 001 LiDAR Topographic Survey Report
Vol.3 coverage polygon or sheet index proving whether Boiling Pot to Mukuni Beach is included
classified ground point cloud or bare-earth DTM for Boiling Pot to Mukuni Beach
survey breaklines and Zambezi main-channel centerline
orthophoto or source-image mosaic with acquisition metadata
cross sections, control points, and vertical-datum transformation metadata
explicit commercial game-use, derivative, screenshot, attribution, storage, and redistribution terms
excluded_primary_products: the 0.3 m dam-break terrain database documented for the downstream Batoka dam-to-Lake Kariba model
the approximately 13 square kilometre 2014 eBee DSM centered on the Batoka dam project area
additional LiDAR attributed to the separate Kariba Dam Rehabilitation Project
minimum_metadata: nominal point density or raster cell size
license, attribution, derivative-use, and redistribution conditions

fallback.procedural_scope: bounded visual detailing may add basalt strata and talus but may not invent or conceal authoritative gorge walls, banks, bed elevations, or centerline alignment
